name: pynput (Python Keyboard and Mouse Control)

auth.notes: OS-level input; macOS requires Accessibility permissions

performance.notes: OS input event subsystem; minimal Python overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Control and monitor keyboard/mouse input. from pynput import keyboard, mouse. with keyboard.Listener(on_press=on_press, on_release=on_release) as listener: listener.join(). Controller: kb = keyboard.Controller(); kb.press('a'); kb.release('a'); kb.type('Hello World'). Mouse: mouse.Controller().move(100, 100); .click(Button.left). Listener callbacks run in separate thread. Special keys: keyboard.Key.ctrl, Key.alt, Key.enter. Hotkeys: with keyboard.GlobalHotKeys({'<ctrl>+<alt>+h': handler}). macOS: Accessibility permission required.

gotchas: macOS requires Accessibility permission in System Preferences
Listeners run in background threads — thread safety required
Security scanners may flag keyboard listeners as keyloggers
Windows UAC may block input injection in elevated processes
best_when: Keyboard shortcuts and hotkeys for desktop automation
avoid_when: When pyautogui already handles the use case
summary: pynput monitors and controls keyboard and mouse input. keyboard.Listener(on_press=cb) for global keyboard hooks. keyboard.Controller().type('Hello'). mouse.Controller().move(x, y).click(Button.left). GlobalHotKeys({'<ctrl>+h': handler}). Thread-based listeners. macOS requires Accessibility permission. LGPL-3.0. Used for automation, macro recorders, accessibility tools, and GUI testing. Security scanners may flag keyboard listeners.
